titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/189

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=461 status=live modified=2021-03-21 12:00:35.741619+00 | translation moa=462 status=live modified=2017-02-24 14:10:11.415155+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis et fidelibus presentibus et futuris has litteras visuris uel audituris Duncanus
Comes de karric salutem in domino . Nouerit vniuersitas vestra quod cum Monachi de Melros mihi annuatim tenerentur in
solucione trium Marcarum duarum scilicet pro terra de Bethoc et vnius pro terra de Auchnefure sicut in eorum cartis conti
netur  Ego easdem tres Marcas prefatis Monachis omnino relaxaui et in perpetuum quietas clamaui pro quadraginta
Marcis esterlingorum quas Idem Monachi simul et semel in magna necessitate mea scilicet pro terra mea in libertatem
ponenda mihi pacauerunt . vnde et Ego et heredes mei et successores mei hanc quietam clamacionem predictarum trium
Marcarum contra omnes homines prefatis monachis in perpetuum warantizabimus . Et ut hec quieta clamacio firma sit et stabilis  posui
testem et fideiussorem Dominum Willelmum Episcopum Glasguens’em . concedens ut si aliquando Ego uel heredes mei uel successores mei contra hoc
uel contra elemosinas eisdem Monachis a me factas prout in cartis meis continetur aliquid attemptauerimus  Idem Episcopus et eius successores
nos ad omnia premissa obseruanda ad instanciam ipsorum monachorum sine cognicione cause super censuram ecclesiasticam compellant .
In cuius rei testimonium et munimen presenti scripto sigillum meum una cum sigillo Domini . W . Episcopi Glasguensis apponi feci . Hiis
Testibus . Domino Waltero filio Alani senescallo Scoc’ . Radulfo de leuint’ Clerico . Alano Milite meo . Alano filio
meo persona de kirchemanen et de stratun . Magistro hugone clerico meo et Multis aliis . Actum anno domini millesimo
ducentesimo tricesimo sexto .

To all sons of holy Mother Church and to the faithful, present and future, who shall view or hear these letters, Duncan, earl of Carrick, sends greeting in the Lord. Know, all of you, that since the monks of Melrose were yearly bound to me in the payment of three merks, that is, two for the land of Beoch and one for the land of Auchnefure, just as it is set out in their charters: I have entirely released and perpetually quitclaimed the same three merks for the aforementioned monks in return for the forty merks sterling which the same monks have paid to me in my great need, that is, for my land to be put at liberty. For this reason, both I and my heirs and successors shall warrant this quitclaim of the aforesaid three merks against all men for the aforementioned monks for ever. And so that this quitclaim should be strong and enduring, I have placed as witness and guarantor the lord William, bishop of Glasgow, allowing that if at any time I or my heirs or successors should try anything against this or against the alms made to the same monks by me as set out in my charters, the same bishop and his successors shall compel us to observe all the foregoing at the request of the monks themselves, without knowledge of the cause, upon pain of ecclesiastical censure. As witness and defence of this matter I have applied my own seal to the present writing together with the seal of the lord William, bishop of Glasgow. With these witnesses: the lord Walter son of Alan, steward of Scotland, Ralph of Livingston, clerk, Alan my knight, Alan my son, parson of Kirchemanen and of Straiton, Master Hugh my clerk, and many others. Done in the year of the Lord 1236.
